messages.content:
Brief reasoning: I can’t run tests from here, so I’ll give the exact steps and commands you can run locally (or in CI) and a checklist for interpreting results and troubleshooting. If you meant a specific “task 6” inside this repository, tell me the test runner and path/name and I’ll give a tailored command or produce a runnable script.

Direct answer — how to run “load test task 6”:

1) Clarify which test you mean
- Do you mean a test file named “task6”, a test case with tag/ID 6, or a load-test scenario called “task 6” in a load tool (k6/Locust/JMeter/etc.)?
- Which runner do you use in this repo (pytest, k6, locust, JMeter, custom script)?
Reply with that and I’ll produce the exact command.

2) Generic run instructions (common cases)
- k6 (JS script named task6.js)
  - Install: download k6 (https://k6.io/docs/getting-started/installation)
  - Run: k6 run path/to/task6.js
  - Example: k6 run tests/load/task6.js

- Locust (Python locustfile with a TaskSet named Task6)
  - Install: pip install locust
  - Run: locust -f path/to/locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 --run-time 5m --host http://TARGET
  - To run a specific TaskSet, either set an environment variable or modify the locustfile to expose only that scenario, or use tags if configured: locust -f locustfile.py --tags task6 ...

- JMeter (test plan .jmx named task6.jmx)
  - Run in non-GUI mode: jmeter -n -t path/to/task6.jmx -l results_task6.jtl -e -o results_report_dir

- pytest with a load plugin or a test named test_task6.py
  - Run: pytest tests/test_task6.py -q
  - If you have a load-testing wrapper, follow its README. Some projects use tox or npm scripts (npm run load-test -- task6).

3) Expected output and pass/fail criteria
- k6: summary at end with checks, requests/second, error rate. Look for low error rate and acceptable latency percentiles (p95, p99).
- Locust: summary lines with total requests, failures, average response time. Check failure count and response times.
- JMeter: check .jtl and generated HTML report; look at throughput, error %, and response time percentiles.
- pytest: tests should exit 0 on success; look for assertion failures or timeouts.

4) Quick checklist before running
- Confirm target service is up and reachable (host/port, auth).
- Set any required environment variables (API keys, DB connection strings).
- Ensure test data exists (seed DB if needed).
- Decide concurrency and duration: do a small smoke run first (low concurrency, short duration).
- Monitor system metrics on the target (CPU, memory, network, disk) during the test.

5) Troubleshooting common issues
- High error rate: check service logs, inspect request payloads and headers, verify auth tokens.
- Timeouts/connect errors: confirm host/port and that load generator network can reach target.
- Resource saturation on load generator: move to multiple generators or increase machine size.
- Tests failing locally but not in CI: check environment variables and config differences.

If you want me to produce a concrete runnable command or CI job for the repository (or a k6/locust/JMeter script) submit which tool and the test file/name/path and I’ll generate the exact command or script. If you want me to actually run it here, note I can’t execute code in this chat — please submit a concrete coding request (or disable Chat-only mode) so I can produce run scripts or CI config.
